IN THE COURT OF HON’BLE JFCM COURT FOR RAILWAYS AT GUNTUR

            Honored Sir, 					                              Date: 03.11.2022

Occurred on 02.11.2022 prior to 18.00 hours and reported on 03.11.2022 at 00.30 hours.  Where in the complainant Sri P.B.Ingle, IPF/RPF/NLG received a information from one passenger by name Sri Venkatramana Mob.No. 7483990948 who was travelling in Tr.No.17221 LTT Express from Rajahmandry to Wadi at 17.20 hours he noticed near Nalgonda Railway station that one trolley bag and one back pack is lying under the seat which is unchained in front side general coach 2nd from engine.  After receiving the information he informed to Sri S. Koteswara Rao, RHC 522 SHO RPS Nalgonda.  Further he sent Sri P. Shiv Shankar, RPC 22 of NLDA for attending the coach.  The RPC 22 and Smt G. Lalitha, W/Con 527 RPF NLDA attended the coach taken the bags and brought to out from the train at NLDA Railway Station.  Further, jointly checked by RPC 22, IPF/NLDA and found (11) bundles wg approximate 19 kgs of Cannabis.  The same informed to SHO/NLDA for further necessary action. 1). Blue  colour Peter Pan Trolley bag containing 7 bundles and 2) Back pack B.K.Bags company containing 4 bundles. 
                                                                                           Sd/- Sri P.B.Ingle, IF/RPF/NLG
Received a Complaint on 03-11-2022 at 00.30 hours.
As per the contents of the above complaint, I S. Koteswara Rao, RHC 522 SHO RPS Nalgonda has registered a case vide in Cr.No.121/2022 U/s 20 (b) (ii) (B)   of NDPS Act & r/w U/s 102 CrPC issued FIR and the case is handed over to Sri M.Venkatram Naik, IRP/Sec’bad Line for further investigation as the case is Special Grave in nature.
                                                                  Sd/- S. Koteswara Rao, RHC 522
                                                                             SHO of RPS Nalgonda.

IN THE COURT OF HONOURABLE JUDICIAL FIRST CLASS MAGISTRATE
FOR RAILWAYS AT KAZIPET
 Honoured Sir/Madam,   

The brief facts of the case are that, Occurred on 28-11-2022 at about 18.00 hours and reported on same day at 19.30 hours by the complainant Sri.B Ramesh, RPC 252 of RPS Kazipet. In which the complainant stated that, as per the instructions of the superior officers on 28-11-2022 he deputed for platform beat duty from 13.00 hours to 21.00 hours. While performing platform beat duty at about 18.45 hours the complainant found (01) bag near escalator on platform No.1 of Kazipet railway station without persons under suspicious circumstances. He asked to nearby passengers but no one came forward to claim. Then he checked the bag found one bundle packed with brown colour tape and he suspeted that bundle might have Ganja. Immediately he informed the same to me/SIRP Kazipet through phone. Immediately I went to kazipet railway station PF No 1 and  conducted seizer panchanama  in presence of G Naresh, IRP Warangal and two mediators by seizing the total (8.5) Kgs Ganja, Total Worth RS.42,500/-
. 
                                            						   Sd/-
                            						             K Ashok Kumar	
                                                            	               SIRP Kazipet.


 Received on 28-11-2022 at 19.30 hours.
As per the above contant, I SIRP of RPS Kazipet registered sue-moto case 
in Cr.No.129/2022 U/s 8(C),20(b) of NDPS Act r/w 102 Cr.P.C and investigation handed over to IRP Kazipet line.

                                                                                    
 				                                                             Sd/-
				                                                  (K.Ashok Kumar, SIRP)
                                                                             		      RPS Kazipet.

IN THE COURT OF II METROPOLITAN MAGISTRATE FOR RAILWAYS, AT SECUNDERABAD.
Honoured Sir,


Facts of the case are that on 26-12-2022 at 08.00 Hrs received a complaint from Sri Master Vishwanath babu S/o Viswanath, Age.40 yrs, Occ:Senior technician in Hyderabad railway station, R/o H.No.1-48, 02, Kothapet, Nageswar colony, Hyderabad-500035. In which he stated that on 25-12-2022 night he along with his staff while on duty, at 23.15 Hrs, the train No.07030 (Agartala – Secunderbad express) was brought to pit line No.10 of railway yard of Hyderabad station. Then he along with K.Swami technician and staff were examining the train coaches, in the mean time the technician Swamy noticed five suspected packets which tied on trolley sole bar of A-1 coach with GI wire and another five suspected packets noticed which tied on trolley sole bar of B-5 coach with GI wire and the technician Swamy informed him the same. Then they removed such packets from trolley sole bar and kept at coach care centre, on observed the packets its smelt like Ganja. Further stated that un known offender transported the Ganja packets through above train from somewhere illegally. Hence he requested to take necessary action in to the matter. 
															                                                                      	Sd/-
							    Sri Vayalapti Ramesh						                                           Cell No.8686229953.
Received On 26.12.2022 at 08:00 Hrs
As per the content of the above petition, I A. Srinivas-IRP of RPS Hyderabad registered a case in Cr.No.218/2022 U/s 20(b)(ii)(B) NDPS Act-1985 & 102 CrPC and took up the investigation 

					                                  Sd/-
        A.Sriniva-IRP
  RPS-Hyderabad.

IN THE COURT OF THE HONOURABLE XI ADDITIONAL CHIEF METROPOLITAN MAGISTRATE AT SECUNDERABAD. 

Honoured Sir,
            
         Brief facts of the case are that today i.e 25.01.2023 at 1800 hrs received a complaint from Sri K. Gangadhar, Sub-Inspector of Police, PS Begumpet, Hyderabad submit that a few lines that on 25.01.2023 at 1500 Hours, while he at police station, he received the credible information that two persons are selling Ganja in the area of Bhagwanthapur. Immediately he informed the same to superior officers. As such he summoned panchas and requested for recording the confessional statement of accused persons. he also secured the presence of Gazetted Officer Smt. D. Debora, Detective Inspector of Police PS Begumpet. he along with Gazetted officer and Panchayathdars namely 1) Sri. Margula Suri, S/o: Late Laxman, Age: 48 Years, Occ: Sanitary Overseer at Secunderabad Cantonment Board, R/o: H.No. 9-3-15/3, Asmanpet, Old Bowenpally, Secunderabad. Mob: 99518 78291 & 2) K. Rama Krishna S/o Chennaiah, age 46 yrs, Occ: Sanitary Overseer at Secunderabad Cantonment Board, R/o H.No.4-8-730/79, Adarsh Nagar, Bollaram, Secunderabad, cell no. 8331002135  conducted raid along with a team namely 1) B. Harish PC 5569 & 2) Shaik Ajju, PC 3160 of P.S. Begumpet rushed to the spot and apprehended said persons 1) Gajula Sai Kumar & 2) Pandena Nikhil and on interrogation they disclosed their identity particulars as 1) Gajula Sai Kumar, S/o: Ashok, Age: 20 Years, Occ: Labour (Welding Work), R/o: H.No. 5-23, Gangannapet, Lakkaram, Adilabad Dist. Mob: 91008 20566, 2) Pandena Nikhil S/o: Srinivas, Age: 20 Years, Occ: Pvt. Job, R/o: H.No. 2-55, Angadi Bazaar, Utnoor, Adilabad Dist. Mob: 99894 50138 and voluntarily admitted their guilt in commission of this offence, then he recorded the confession cum seizure panchanama and seized 03 Packets of 9.301 Grams Ganja, 05 Strips of Nitravet 10 Mg Tablets & 02 Bottles of Eskuf Syrup & One Realme mobile from the possession of Gajula Sai Kumar and seized 03 Packets of 8.143 Grams Ganja and 01 Nokia basic mobile phone from the possession of Pandena Nikhil. Both are addicted to smoking of Ganja on enquiry they revealed that they are procuring Ganja from un-known person at Nampally, Hyderabad. Hence he request to take necessary legal action on the above said persons as per law.

Sd/-
Sri K. Gangadhar.
Phn; 8712660274.

Received on 25.01.2023 at 1800hrs.
                  As per the contents of the above petition I P.Srinivas Rao, Inspector of Police, PS Begumpet registered a case in Cr.No.51/2023 U/s 20(b)(ii)(A) rw8(c) of NDPS Act and took up Investigation.

Sd/-
Sri P.Srinivas, 
Inspector of Police.
PS Begumpet, Hyderabad.

IN THE COURT OF HONOURABLE JUDICIAL FIRST CLASS MAGISTRATE FOR
RAILWAYS AT  KAZIPET

Honoured Sir,   
                     Today i.e. on 04.02.2023 at 13.10 hours I received a petition from Sri. Ch. Parashuramulu  SIRP of RPS, Warangal which runs as follows.
           The facts of the case are that on 04.02.2023 morning hours, as per the instructions of superior officers, the complainant Sri. Ch. Parashuramulu, SIRP RPS/WL along with staff        Sri. M. Nagaraju, RPC-620 and Smt. I. Sharada, WRPC-90 of RPS,Warangal and RPF HC-601,    J. Krishnakar of RPF Post. Warangal have conducted checking & frisking on platforms of Warangal Railway station, while on checking on platform No. 2 towards Chinthal end, in the meantime the Tr.No. 18519 LTT Express (from VSKP to Lokmanya Tilak Terminus) arrived on platform No. 2 of Warangal Railway station and stopped due to not clearing the route, Upon which, he along with his staff have boarded in Tr. No. 18519 in backside of general coach, while on checking where found one black colour shoulder bag on its name of Skaybags was placed on luggage stand under suspicious circumstances, when checked that shoulder bag which is some smell like GANJA is coming out from the bag, made an enquiry about this bag with the passengers available in the coach, but no body come to forward to claim that bag and got alighted the shoulder bag from the train. Immediately he passed the above information to his superior officers. Later, he addressed a letter to Inspector, Probation and Excise, Fort-Warangal to present for conducting panchanama.  On that Sri. B. Chandra Mohan, Inspector, Probation and Excise, Fort-Warangal has visited to the scene of offence at Warangal Railway station on platform No. 2 in the presence of independent with two panchas viz. 1). Mekiri Ramesh S/o Shivaiah, age 49 yrs, Caste. Arekhetriya Occ: Railway canteen-kitchen works R/o Anantharam (v), Parvathagiri (mdl) of Warangal district  and 2). Mittapalli Sravan S/o Ramesh, age 25 yrs, Caste. SC-Madiga Occ: Cycle stand worker R/o H. No. 16-9-921, A.C. Reddy Nagar, Shivanagar-Warangal.  Later, he checked the shoulder bag found  (5) brown color packets contains each DRY-GANJA. Later, he  weighted the DRY-GANJA with the help of Syed Nazeer, Supervisor of K& S Stall, which is located on platform No. 2 of Warangal Railway station, weighed the (5) brown color packets contains DRY-GANJA, each packet wg  2-KGs (Total 10-Kgs of DRY-GANJA). Later, he conducted seizure panchanama before the mediators and Inspector, Probation & Excise, Fort-Warangal under cover of panchanama. Then he brought the seized case property i.e. black color shoulder bag contains DRY-GANJA  to RPS, Warangal and handing over to me for taking necessary action.
                                                                                                                                           Sd/-
                                                                                                                     Sri. Ch. Parashuramulu, SIRP
                                                                                                                          RPS, Warangal.
Received on 04.02.2023 at 13.10  hours. 
             As per the contents of the above report & Seizure panchanama, 
 I registered a case in Cr.No. 18/2023 U/s 8 (c), 20 (b) of NDPS Act 
and 102 Cr.P.C., and took up the investigation.

                                                                                          Sd/-
                                                                       Inspector of Railway Police,
                                                                                 RPS Warangal.

IN THE COURT OF II METROPOLITAN MAGISTRATE FOR RAILWAYS, AT SECUNDERABAD.

Honoured Sir,
Facts of the case are that on 01-04-2023 at 17.00 Hrs received a complaint from Sri. Valaparla Ranga Rao S/o  V.Bullaiah, Age 32 years, Occupation-Sub Inspector of Police RPF Hyderabad(post), R/O H.No. 6-1-401/32/D,CIB quarters, khairathabad, Hyderabad of Telangana state,-500004.  He was SI Working in Hyderabad RPF to day on 01.04.2023 at about 16:00hrs on duty head constable 5569 Sri D.V.Reddy, RPF Hyderabad given information regarding one red color bag was found at platform numbers.05 and 06 Hyderabad dead end of Hyderabad railway station for past one hour. On receipt of information  he rushed to spot and noticed one red color bag and informed the same to Sri Sai kumar RHC-15 of RPS Hyderabad who is on crime prevention duty. After some time they opend the bag and found (08) no’s of packets sealed with plaster and it smells like ganja. Immediately he came to the RPS Hyderabad to lodge complaint. Hence the complainant requested to take necessary action in to the matter.
                                   Sd/- 
                    Valaparla Ranga Rao
	          Cell No.9701371732.

Received On 01.04.2023 at 17:00 hrs
As per the content of the above petition, I A. Srinivas-IRP of RPS Hyderabad registered a case in Cr.No.71/2023 U/s 20(b)(ii)(B) NDPS ACT and took up the investigation

                                                                    A.Sriniva-IRP
                                                                    RPS-Hyderabad.

IN THE COURT OF 3rd ADDL JUDICIAL FIRST CLASS MAGISTRATE AT KOTHAGUDEM
Honored Sir,
Occurred on 02.04.2023 at 08.00 hrs at Regina School, Paloncha and reported on 02.04.2023 at 12.00 hrs the Brief facts of the case are that, on 02.04.2023 at 08.00 hours the complainant G. Praveen Kumar, SI of Police, Paloncha Town PS received a reliable information that on 02.04.2023 at 08.00hrs Marepalli Anil Kumar S/o Eeliya, 43yrs, Madiga, FRO at Paloncha R/o BAE-36, KTPS C Colony, Paloncha along with his driver Bhukya Ramesh S/o Ramkoti were conducting patrolling from the Paloncha to Peddamma Temple, Meantime when they reached at Regina School one car TATA INDIGO ECS AP27AG7824 were moving suspiciously, while seeing that driver Ramesh moved the vehicle near to them, on seeing immediately two persons where ran away from the spot by leaving the CAR, Later while they checked the car and noticed 6 bags, in that some packets surrounded by brown colour tape, later intimated the same to the complainant. Immediately complainant along with the staff went to the spot and identified TATA INDIGO ECS AP27AG7824, bearing Chassis No.MAT607161CWC23740, Engine No. 12M2102760 after checking 6 white colour bags which consist of dry ganja, after weighing totally 158 Kgs of dry ganja which is worth of Rs.31,60,000/-, later inside the Car one Blue colour VIVO Cellphone and Airtel sim card also identified immediately seized under cover of panchanama before the secured mediators.

IN THE COURT OF HON’BLE IX ADDL METROPOLITAN MAGISTRATE MEDCHAL MALKAJKGIRI AT MEDCHAL
HONOURED SIR,
On 07.04.2023 at 1300 Hours received complaint from Sri A. Muraleedhar Sub-inspector of Police, Medchal PS. 
Brief Facts of the Case: 
Facts of the case are that on 07.04.2023 at 1300 hours received a complainant from Sri A. Muraleedhar Sub-inspector of Police, Medchal PS  in which she stated that on 07.04.2023 in the morning 09:30 hours he received a credible information regarding illegal selling of banned sunstance ganja near Saint claret school , Basuragadi, Medchal, Upon the persmission of inspector of police he took patrol vehicle staff PC-259 K.V.L Narayana Redddy, HG-2020 Ravinder and also Panchas 1) Sri Bingi Srinivas 2) Sri Palarigayala Bikshapathi and also a gazette officer B.N.S Reddy Inspector of Medchal Traffic along with him to Saint claret school , Basuragadi, Medchal.He found two persons on the BT Road, Saint claret school , Basuragadi, Medchal. In that one of the person seen them and escaped from the spot and the other person was caught by the complainant and the enquired the person then the person revealed his name and details as  Ravi Laxman Yadav S/o: Laxman Yadav, Age:34 Years, Occ: Welding Work(Megha Engineering Company),R/o: Gnanpur Village, Medchal N/o: Chatar Village, Kucchila Post, Rohtas District, Bihar state.Cell:9798285927. As per the NDPS Act guidelines to the search and seizure the person he asked to hire any known gazette officer then the accused person said he don’t any . then the complainant replied they have one gazette officer if there is no problem for the accused they can search him in the presence of the gazette officer. The accused person accepted and signed on the Option notice. After that the Gazetted officer B.N.S Reddy along with the panchas conducted search of the accused person and opened the black polythelene cover and found some wet and dry leaves and also some flower kind of material was found in that 5 small packets containing leaves and flower kind of material was filled. That substance are emitting strong pungent smell and they asked the accused person what are those he replied that it is ganja. The material in the black polythelene cover and the 5 small packets containing the material were placed into another black polythelene cover. They weighed the material on the KIT which was carried by the complainant and obtained  378 grams. The mouth of the black cover was tied with a thread and marked on it as M1, 5 small packets, empty black cover were placed into an another polythelene cover and marked it as P1. On those M1, P1 the panchas, gazette officer, accused person and complainant signatures were taken and affixed the signed chits by following the procedure invold in NDPS act and seized the M1 & P1. Hence he requested to take necessary action.
Received on 07.04.2023 at 1300 hours 
Basing on the contents of above petition registered a Case in Cr. No. 237/2023 U/s 20(b)(ii)(a) of NDPS Act of PS Medchal and entrusted the investigation to CH.Raghuramulu SIP.                          
						

                                                                                                                 Inspector of Police, 
  								                  PS Medchal Cyberabad

IN THE COURT OF THE ADDITIONAL JUDICIAL FIRST-CLASS MAGISTRATE AT RAMANNAPET
Honored Sir,
	The facts of the case are that on 28.05.2023 at 14:00 hours  Sri M.Laxmaiah, Sub-Inspector of Police, PS Ramannapet, Rachakonda received reliable information that, some persons are transporting Ganja illegally, on believing said information to be true and authentic, SIP Ramannapet PS have informed the same to superior officer and sent requisition to Tahsildar, Ramannapet with a request to depute (2) employees to act as panchas, then secured the presence of panchas and explained them about the information received and requested them to act as witness for the official proceedings, for which they agreed and the SI of Police along with Sri. V.Anjaneyulu, Tahsildar Ramannapet Mandal and two official mediators 1) Akkenepally Nagesh kumar, 2) Devarai Sai Kumar and police staff PCs 4449, 3779, 4357 were proceeded to Ambedkar Statue at Ramannapet village & mandal  on Chityala to Valigonda road and reached there.  

 	 While waiting for suspected vehicle, at about 17:00 hours police were found one KIA seltos car bearing No.MH-25-AW-6615 (which was suspected by the Police) proceeding towards Bhongir via Chityala, the police party stopped said vehicle, but is driver along with inmates were tried to escape from place, the police caught hold them and asked their identity. They revealed their identity as A-1) Raju Shinde S/o Ambadas shinde, age: 30 yrs, Caste: ST Pardhi, Occ: Driver, R/o Manduva village, Washi (TQ), Osmanabad Dist, Maharashtra State. Cell no: 8459104106, A-2) Sanjay Chawhan S/o Ravinder Chawhan, age: 25yrs, Caste: Rajputh, occ: Catering, R/o Barshi nagar chouk, Osmanabad Dist& Town, Maharashtra,Cel no: 9770020324.A-3) Shravan Chunnilal Sharma S/o Chunnilal, age: 24 yrs, Caste: OC(Badhai)  Occ: Catering, r/o: H.No.301,Parasnadh building,Naigaon east Star city, Mumbai, Maharashtra Cell:8689868929. A-4) Rahul Pintu Shinde S/o Pintu, age: 21 yrs, Caste: ST Pardhi, Occ: Agriculture, r/o Manduva village, Washi(M), Usmanabad(D),  Maharashtra(S). 

 	After that, while searching the dickey of car bearing No. MH-25-AW-6615 and found 60 pockets contains Ganja are in same in colour sealed with brown color tape, which is Narcotic Drug under the NDPS Act, 1985 and illegally transporting the Ganja pockets in the vehicle, on questioning about the material the inmates of car replied in negative and feigned ignorance. On questioning by SI of Police, they replied that as per instructions Mukthar, they are illegally transporting from Vishakapatnam to Mumbaia for selling to required customers in order to earn money in easy and illegal manner.   
 	
 	They are spoken in the Hindi language, then; SI secured the Hindi translator by name Meerga Inayath baig and called photographer Karne Vinay and weighing shop owner Appam Upender for weighing the Ganja. 

 	In the presence of Tahsildar, SI Ramannapet the said two panchas were  60 sachets weighted by weighing shop owner Appam Upender, each pocket of Ganja having 2Kgs weight total weighing about 120 Kgs, which was found in the KIA seltos car bearing No. MH-25-AW-6615.  

 	SI cut opened the all packets and found and confirmed that is Ganja, SI made a homegenious mix of all packets and all packets ganja filled six bags and it marked as M-1 to M-6 for identification, the empty two polythene packets are packed in a white color polythene cover and it marked as P-1 for identification, M-1 to M-6 & P-1 weighing about 120 Kgs, it was measured by the Appam Upender.

 	 Later, SI recorded the confession- cum-seizure panchanama and seized M-1 to M-6, P-1, and one car bearing MH-25-AW-6615 and one mobile phone of VIVO company black colour  were seized from possession of A-1) Raju Shinde. Subsequently, SI seized one mobile phone of Redmi, white colony from possession of A-2) Sanjay Chawhan, seized one mobile  xiomi (redmi) company, light pink was seized from A-4) Rahul Pintu Shinde  by affixed panchas signed chits on its.
 	The accused confessed that, A-1 to A-4 were agreed with A-5 to transport the Ganja from Vishakapatnam to Mumbai. Accordingly, A-5 has paid advance an amount of Rs 30,000/- to A-1 to A-4 and also agreed to pay 70,000/- after delivered Ganja at Mumbai.  On 24.05.2023 as per directions of A-5) Mukthar the A-1 to A-4 were left from Osmanabad District of Maharashtra State and they went to Vishakapatnam on their car bearing no. MH-25-AW-6615. They visited cite seeing locations around Vishakapatnam and they spend their. On 27.05.2023 they met with one unknown person (mobile no. 6371318044) at Araku, Vishakapatnam area and get 60 packets of ganja in their car dickey, each packet weighing about 2 kgs (total weighing 120 Kgs). The Ganja amount was paid by the Mukthar. On 27.05.2023 in the night time, they left from Vishakapathnm and return to go to Mumbai via Vijayawada, Suryapet, Chital,  Gajwel, Nizamabad, Maharastra. On the way today i.e. on 28.5.2023 at about 17:00hrs they reached at Ramannapet village, in the meantime the police Ramannapet  was apprehended them along with car. 

Later at 22:15 hours the accused along with above seized material were brought to police station for taking necessary action.

Returned to PS on 28.05.2023 at 22:15 hours:

          Basing on the contents of above confession cum seizure panchanama, I, M.Laxmaiah, SIP registered a suo-motto case in Cr.No.113/2023 U/sec 8 (C) r/w 20 (B)(ii)(C) of Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985 and took up the investigation.
Hence the FIR.
Sd/-

M.Laxmaiah
Sub-Inspector of police 
P.S. Ramannapet.

IN THE COURT OF HON’BLE IX ADDL METROPOLITAN MAGISTRATE MEDCHAL MALKAJKGIRI AT MEDCHAL
HONOURED SIR,
On 18.06.2023 at 0200 hours received petition from Sri. B. Bhoopal Goud Sub-Inspector of Police, Medchal Traffic PS, Cell: 8712663502.
Brief Facts of the Case: 
Facts of the case are that on 18.06.2023 at 0200 hours received a complaint from Sri. B. Bhoopal Goud Sub-Inspector of Police, Medchal Traffic PS, in which he stated that on 17.06.2023 at 1030 hrs, he along with Sachidanand ASI, G. Shivanandam PC-4270 and MD. Faheemuddin ARPC-7173 were performing drunk and drive test to the motorist at Medchal Checkpost, who were proceeding from ORR to Medchal side. At about 2340 hrs, when they stopped a Scorpio vehicle, the driver and another person who sat beside the driver left the vehicle there itself and escaped from there. He tried to catch them, but efforts went in vain. After, he inspected the vehicle, then he found small brown color bags in the back seat of the said vehicle. pungent smell was coming from the said bags. One of the bags had a tiny hole, small green colored leaves came out from that hole. He suspected them as Ganja leaves. And the vehicle registration is Br. No. DL12CA4981. He found a yellow color ID card beside Hand break which is next to driver seat and “Driver passport” was mentioned on that ID card and also photo is affixed to it. And also mentioned on the ID card “Name-Nikhil Kunnal Singh, ACJ Transport, Driver ID-20285653, Validity Date-31.03.2023, Blood Group-O+, Age: 27 yrs”. Immediately, he informed the same to the Inspector of Police, traffic, Medchal PS, B.N.S. Reddy. As per the instructions of B.N.S Reddy, Inspector he came to PS and requested to take necessary action. He deputed his staff as the escort to vehicle Br. No. DL12CA4981 and came to PS. Hence he requested to take necessary action.
Received on 18.06.2023 at 0200 hours
Basing on the contents of above petition I, Rajashekar Reddy, Inspector, registered a Case in Cr. NO. 420/2023 U/s 20 (b)(ii)(C) of NDPS Act and took up the investigation. 

                                                                                                                          Inspector of Police, 
  						 		                                                     PS Medchal Cyberabad
